target.name: Orangewood Labs
target.founder: Abhinav Das, Aditya Bhatia, Akash Bansal

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: SendCutSend
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Orangewood Labs is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company founded by Abhinav Das, Aditya Bhatia, and Akash Bansal. The company has raised $5M+ to date. The robotics and manufacturing sector includes one public comparable (277810.KQ) and eight private peers, including Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity. However, the peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables at similar valuation tiers to support reliable median-based triangulation.

Given data constraints, stage-progression analysis is the primary applicable method. Using $10M as a proxy for total capital raised, a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier from Series A forward yields an implied valuation of $20M. This methodology applies conventional stage advancement dynamics in frontier tech but does not incorporate company-specific unit economics, market position, or differentiation.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $17M to $23M, with a median point of $20M. This range reflects conservative application of a single method with inherent limitations. Key uncertainties include the absence of granular peer comparables at equivalent stage and tier, which prevents cross-validation via market multiples. Resolution of these uncertainties would require either narrower peer identification within Robotics & Manufacturing or disclosure of financial metrics enabling revenue or growth-based valuation approaches. The range should be viewed as preliminary pending additional comparable data.
